description: Allow the `?` operator anywhere an expression can appear (call arguments, binary/index operands, return payloads), hoisting each nested `?` out of its statement while preserving left-to-right evaluation order.

userStories.title: Hoist nested ? in expression position
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `?` to work when it is nested inside an expression (call argument, binary operand, return payload) so that I do not have to hand-lift every `?` onto its own line.
acceptanceCriteria: The emitter transpiles `f(g()?)`, `g()? + 1`, `return Result.Ok(f(g()?))`, `add(g()?, h()?)`, a chained `outer(g()?)?`, and a nested Option `?` (`f(find(x)?)`) instead of failing with `unsupported expression *ast.UnwrapExpr`.

Each nested `?` reuses the existing statement-position unwrap lowering: the emitted Go contains the same `tmp, err := …; if err != nil { return … }` (Result) or `o := …; if o == nil { return nil }` (Option) form the top-level `?` already produces, bound to a fresh gensym.

New corpus golden pairs (`*.goal` + `*.go.expected`) under the 05-question-prop examples directory cover single-nested, binary-operand, return-nested, multiple-`?`, chained-`?`, and Option-nested shapes, and `go test ./internal/corpus -count=1` passes with them.
`go run ./cmd/goal check` on a file that uses nested `?` reports zero `question-*` diagnostics for these valid cases.
userStories.notes: A proven reference implementation exists in the working tree (internal/backend/emit.goal: `hoisted` field, expr() substitution, hoistStmt/hoistRoots/hoistExpr, UnwrapExpr expr-case). After editing emit.goal you MUST run `task generate` so the committed internal/backend/emit.go matches, or verify-generated fails. After adding example files run `task update-goldens` then `task corpus-gen` to bless the goldens/manifest; the gate then verifies them. This story covers straight-line hoisting only — evaluation ordering of side effects is US-002.

userStories.title: Preserve evaluation order for hoisted ?
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need side-effecting operands that are evaluated before a nested `?` to keep their source order so that hoisting the `?` never changes program behaviour.
acceptanceCriteria: For `add(plain(), g()?)` the emitted Go binds `plain()` to a temp BEFORE the `g()?` temp (source order preserved), and for `add3(a()?, plain(), b()?)` the emitted temps appear in source order a, plain, b.
Corpus golden pairs capture the ordered lowering for both cases above and `go test ./internal/corpus -count=1` passes.
Operands that evaluate no function call (integer/string literals, `x + 1`, field reads) are NOT hoisted: the emitted Go for `f(1, g()?)` binds no temp for `1`.
The verify-generated stage stays green, i.e. the committed generated Go for all `?`-free code in `./internal` is byte-identical after this change.
userStories.notes: Reference implementation: the ANF-prefix pass in emit.goal (hoistArgs binds call-valued operands preceding the last `?`-carrying operand; containsUnwrap/containsCall predicates; `hoisted` keyed by ast.Expr). Only bind operands that contain a call, so pure expressions stay inline and `?`-free output is unchanged. Re-run `task generate` after editing emit.goal.

userStories.title: Reject ? on short-circuit && / || side
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need a clear error when I place `?` on the conditionally-evaluated side of `&&` or `||` so that I am never silently miscompiled by unconditional hoisting.
acceptanceCriteria: Transpiling `cond && (g()? > 0)` fails with a diagnostic that names the short-circuit restriction (a message distinct from the generic `unsupported expression %T`).
A `?` inside a `match` arm body or a function literal is NOT hoisted above the enclosing statement: a corpus golden shows the arm-local / closure-local `?` lowering unchanged from today.

A "Rejected with" error block is added to the 05-question-prop by-example markdown for the `&& g()?` case, and `go test ./internal/byexample -count=1` validates that the recorded error matches the emitter's actual message.
userStories.notes: The hoist walk must not descend into MatchExpr or FuncLit (they own their `?`). Reference: the LAND/LOR branch of hoistExpr calls e.fail when the RHS contains a `?`. Re-run `task generate` after editing emit.goal.

userStories.title: Document nested ? in feature docs
userStories.description: As a goal learner, I need the by-example docs and the generated guide to show nested `?` so that the new capability is discoverable and machine-verified.
acceptanceCriteria: `features/05-question-prop/TRANSPILE.md` gains a "nested `?`" section with at least one "Transpiles to" goal-to-Go pair covering `f(g()?)` and one covering the evaluation-order case, and `go test ./internal/byexample -count=1` parses and validates them against live transpilation.
The generated guide includes the nested-`?` example and `go test ./internal/guidetest -count=1` passes.
userStories.notes: By-example blocks are transpiled live by the test binary, so the Go in each block must exactly match current emitter output.

userStories.title: Regenerate AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md refreshed after the new nested-`?` examples and short-circuit rejection so that generated agent context stays accurate and does not drift.
acceptanceCriteria: AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md is regenerated with `go run ./cmd/goal ai > AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md` and reflects the nested-`?` capability and the short-circuit rejection.
Re-running `go run ./cmd/goal ai` and diffing against the committed AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md produces no differences (the committed copy is up to date).
userStories.notes: Per project memory, by-example / error features require regenerating AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md. The generator is `goal ai`; the file header documents the regeneration command.
